_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: History of mid-trimester spontaneous previous abortion (from 16 weeks of pregnancy onwards): painless cervical dilatation and escape of amniotic fluid followed by painless expulsion of the products of conception
Present pregnancy: Transvaginal ultrasound scan carried out between 16 and 24 weeks of pregnancy that show a cervical length of 25 mm or less.
Present pregnancy: During antenatal examination, dilated cervix and exposed, unruptured fetal membranes between 16 and 28 weeks of pregnancy
Examination findings during non-pregnant state: Bimanual examination reveal presence of unilateral or bilateral cervical tear
Examination findings during non-pregnant state: Passage number 6-8 Hegar dilator beyond the internal os without any resistance
Examination findings during non-pregnant state: Postmenstrual hysterocervicography shows funnel-shaped shadow.

clinical_thresholds.note: Should be less than or equal to for cerclage, as >4cm is a contraindication

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, indications & examination findings
Investigations such as USG report (if available)
Antenatal record of current pregnancy, if available

claim.label: Investigation reports including detailed USG scan
Detailed discharge summary, including advice on getting the cerclage removal at 37th week

ppd_preauth.q: Is a USG scan available ruling out congenital malformation in the foetus?
Are there any signs of bleeding per vaginum?

common_queries: USG scan is not available or does not rule out congenital malformation in the foetus.
Signs of bleeding per vaginum are present.
Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, indications, and examination findings are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Antenatal record of current pregnancy is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed procedural/operative notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Discharge summary is missing or does not include advice on cerclage removal at 37th week at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: Prophylactic cerclage: done around 14 weeks of pregnancy or at least 2 weeks earlier than the lowest period of previous pregnancy loss.
Elective cerclage done when the cervix is dilated and there is bulging of the membranes.
The stitch should be removed at 37th week or earlier if labor pain starts or features of abortion appear.
quality_assessment_parameters: Proportion of Cerclage done in the hospital to the number of Deliveries performed in the hospital?
Proportion of Cerclage done in the hospital to the number of high-risk pregnancies delivered in the hospital?
contraindications: Scan showing congenital anomaly in the foetus
Severe uterine irritability indicated by periodic uterine contractions
